scope_creep.notes: Corpus stays tightly within the 'When to use RAG' decision-framework boundary. Covers the four signals (freshness, private data, precision, cost-benefit) and the RAG vs fine-tuning distinction — all explicitly within scope. Section 5.4 RAG vs fine-tuning comparison table uses only prior-introduced concepts (vector database, parametric memory) to inform the architectural decision; this is in-scope elaboration, not teaching vector DB internals or pipeline mechanics. Section 6 applies the decision framework to a retail scenario (on-scope). No agent architecture, no vector DB internals, no retrieval pipeline mechanics taught here. The one mention of 'prompt engineering' in Section 6 is a single named-and-deferred alternative, not a teaching block.

verdict: PASS — all six checks pass; one soft note: estimated word count (~2840) is ~5% below the 3000-word standard-profile floor but within the 30% hard-fail threshold; builder may add ~160 words to Section 5 or 7 examples to clear the floor cleanly.
